WorkerProfile.title: WorkerProfile
WorkerProfile.description: Represents the profile and personal information of a gig worker, including medical details and registered platforms for the GuardianRide app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkerProfile entity.

phoneNumberHash.description: Hashed phone number of the worker for identification while maintaining privacy. (Note: Original phone number is not stored directly for security).

name.description: Full name of the gig worker.

medicalConditions.description: Known medical conditions of the worker. This field is sensitive and should be encrypted at rest.

allergies.description: Known allergies of the worker. This field is sensitive and should be encrypted at rest.

currentMedications.description: Medications currently being taken by the worker. This field is sensitive and should be encrypted at rest.

platformIds.description: References to Platform entities the worker is registered with. (Relationship: WorkerProfile N:N Platform)

homeLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the worker's home or base location.

homeLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the worker's home or base location.

registeredAt.description: Timestamp when the worker registered their profile.

EmergencyContact.title: EmergencyContact
EmergencyContact.description: Details of an emergency contact for a gig worker, notified by GuardianRide in case of an incident.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyContact entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile this contact belongs to. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N EmergencyContact)

phoneNumber.description: Phone number of the emergency contact.

relationship.description: Relationship of the contact to the worker (e.g., 'Spouse', 'Parent', 'Friend').

languagePreference.description: Preferred language for communication with this emergency contact (e.g., 'Hindi', 'English').

priorityOrder.description: Priority order for contacting this person during an emergency (e.g., 1 for first contact, 2 for second).

Platform.title: Platform
Platform.description: Represents a gig-work platform (e.g., Swiggy, Zomato) that workers are associated with and which can receive incident notifications from GuardianRide.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Platform entity.

name.description: The name of the gig-work platform (e.g., 'Swiggy', 'Zomato').

webhookUrl.description: The URL endpoint for sending automated incident notifications to this platform.

contactEmail.description: Primary contact email for the platform's safety team.

WorkSession.title: WorkSession
WorkSession.description: Records a specific period when a gig worker is actively working and being monitored by the GuardianRide app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkSession entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile who initiated this work session. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N WorkSession)

platformId.description: Reference to the Platform entity the worker was actively working for during this session. (Relationship: Platform 1:N WorkSession)

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the work session began.

endedAt.description: Timestamp when the work session ended.

totalDistanceKm.description: Total distance covered by the worker during this session, in kilometers.

incidentOccurred.description: Indicates if an incident was detected during this work session.

Incident.title: Incident
Incident.description: Details about a detected accident or potential emergency event involving a gig worker, managed by GuardianRide.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Incident entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile involved in the incident. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N Incident)

sessionId.description: Reference to the WorkSession during which the incident occurred. (Relationship: WorkSession 1:N Incident)

detectedAt.description: Timestamp when the system detected the potential incident.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the incident location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the incident location.

speedAtImpactKmph.description: Estimated speed of the worker at the moment of impact, in kilometers per hour.

confidenceScore.description: Confidence score (0-1) from the AI model indicating the likelihood of an actual accident.

confirmedAccident.description: True if the incident was confirmed as an actual accident (either by worker non-response or manual confirmation).

falseAlarm.description: True if the worker manually cancelled the emergency protocol, indicating a false alarm.

emergencyTriggered.description: True if the full emergency protocol (calls, messages) was triggered.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the incident was marked as resolved.

outcome.description: The reported outcome of the incident (e.g., 'fine', 'hospitalised', 'deceased', 'unknown').

EmergencyAction.title: EmergencyAction
EmergencyAction.description: Records a specific action taken as part of the emergency protocol for an incident by GuardianRide.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyAction entity.

incidentId.description: Reference to the Incident this action belongs to. (Relationship: Incident 1:N EmergencyAction)

actionType.description: The type of emergency action performed (e.g., 'CALL_WORKER', 'CALL_CONTACT1', 'CALL_CONTACT2', 'CALL_108', 'NOTIFY_PLATFORM').

triggeredAt.description: Timestamp when this emergency action was triggered.

responseReceived.description: Indicates if a response was received for this action (e.g., call answered, webhook acknowledged).

responseTimeSeconds.description: Time taken to receive a response for the action, in seconds.

AccidentHeatmapData.title: AccidentHeatmapData
AccidentHeatmapData.description: Aggregated and anonymized data representing accident hotspots, used for public safety reports and traffic analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for this aggregated heatmap data point/cell.

gridCellId.description: A unique identifier for the geospatial grid cell this data represents.

latitude.description: Representative latitude of the aggregated grid cell.

longitude.description: Representative longitude of the aggregated grid cell.

incidentCount.description: The total number of incidents recorded in this grid cell during the specified period.

timeSlot.description: The time slot during which incidents are aggregated (e.g., 'morning', 'afternoon', 'night').

weatherCondition.description: The predominant weather condition during the incidents in this aggregation (e.g., 'clear', 'rainy', 'foggy').

platformName.description: The name of the platform associated with these aggregated incidents, or 'all' if aggregated across platforms.

roadType.description: The type of road where incidents occurred (e.g., 'highway', 'urban_street', 'residential').

definition.description: A worker's personal and medical profile. Accessible only by the authenticated worker matching `userId`. The `userId` is denormalized into the path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user (worker) to whom this profile belongs.

definition.description: Emergency contacts for a specific worker. Accessible only by the authenticated worker matching `userId`. The `workerId` is denormalized into the path and also explicitly stored in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user (worker) to whom these contacts belong.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique identifier for an individual emergency contact.

definition.description: Master list of gig-work platforms. Globally readable by all users. The `platformId` is denormalized into the path.

params.name: platformId
params.description: The unique identifier for a gig-work platform.

definition.description: Records work sessions for a specific worker. Accessible only by the authenticated worker matching `userId`. The `workerId` is denormalized into the path and also explicitly stored in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user (worker) who initiated this session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for an individual work session.

definition.description: Details of detected incidents for a specific worker. Accessible only by the authenticated worker matching `userId`. The `workerId` is denormalized into the path and also explicitly stored in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user (worker) involved in this incident.

params.name: incidentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an individual incident.

definition.description: Records specific emergency actions taken for a given incident. Accessible only by the authenticated worker whose `userId` matches the path. The `incidentId` and `workerId` are denormalized into the path, and `incidentId` is also stored in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user (worker) related to this emergency action.

params.name: incidentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the incident to which this action belongs.

params.name: actionId
params.description: The unique identifier for an individual emergency action.

definition.description: Aggregated and anonymized accident data for public reports and analysis. Globally readable by all users. The `heatmapId` is denormalized into the path.

params.name: heatmapId
params.description: The unique identifier for an aggregated heatmap data point.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes strict ownership, clear authorization boundaries, and adherence to the core design principles. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This design inherently achieves **Authorization Independence** (Mandate A) for private data. For instance, to access a worker's profile at `/users/{userId}/profile/main`, the security rule can simply verify `request.auth.uid == userId`. No `get()` operations are needed to check parent document ownership, making rules simple, atomic, and robust.

**Structural Segregation** (Mandate B) is applied by having dedicated top-level collections for global, publicly accessible data (`/platforms`, `/accidentHeatmapData`) and separate, user-owned hierarchies for private data. This ensures that documents within a given collection always share the same security posture, simplifying rule sets.

**Access Modeling** (Mandate C) leverages path-based ownership extensively. For `WorkerProfile`, a dedicated subcollection `profile` containing a `main` document under the user's ID (`/users/{userId}/profile/main`) stores the primary profile. Related entities like `EmergencyContact`, `WorkSession`, and `Incident` are also structured as subcollections directly under the worker's path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/emergencyContacts/{contactId}`). Further nested data, such as `EmergencyAction`, follows this hierarchy (`/users/{userId}/incidents/{incidentId}/emergencyActions/{actionId}`). In each of these private collections, the `workerId` is implicitly denormalized into the path itself. While the `workerId` is also present within the document body for backend query efficiency, the primary authorization check will be against the path wildcard.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** are supported by this structure. For private data, client-side queries for `list` operations must always include the authenticated user's `uid` in the path (e.g., a query for `/users/khEKRKKCjBa6fCbEIPRrXTY1c7s2/workSessions`). This allows security rules to enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, ensuring that the database only returns documents explicitly owned by the requesting user without having to filter results. For public collections like `/platforms` and `/accidentHeatmapData`, `list` operations are permitted for all users, as these collections are designed for broad visibility.
